fundamentals.name: ASML Holding N.V. - New York Re

judgment.summary: ASML exhibits strong profitability and growth, but carries a premium valuation and significant leverage.
judgment.earningsRecap: ASML reported an EPS of $7.15 for the quarter ending March 31, 2026, beating the estimate of $6.62 by 8.0%. The next earnings date is July 15, 2026.
positives: Profit margin of 29.7% and operating margin of 36.0% indicate strong operational efficiency.
Return on equity at 52.2% demonstrates excellent profitability relative to shareholder equity.
Revenue growth of 13.2% and earnings growth of 19.2% show solid top and bottom-line expansion.
Free cash flow of $8.24B (TTM) provides substantial operational cash generation.
Analyst consensus is a strong buy with a mean target price suggesting upside potential.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 51.57 and Price/Book of 1357.86 suggest a high valuation.
Forward P/E of 32.50, while lower than trailing, still implies significant growth expectations.
Debt/Equity ratio of 12.99 indicates a high level of financial leverage.
Quick ratio of 0.69 suggests potential short-term liquidity challenges if inventory cannot be sold quickly.
Beta of 1.37 implies higher volatility than the overall market.

news.paragraph: ASML Holding N.V. (ASML) has seen significant positive news flow following solid recent results, with its CEO indicating a tight supply in the booming chip market driven by soaring AI demand. Analysts at UBS have named ASML a top pick, raising their price target and highlighting an attractive risk/reward profile within European semiconductors. The company's progress on its High NA EUV technology is also drawing attention, though some reports suggest this milestone is testing valuation and growth expectations.
